name: Polopos y Sorvilán – circular de los almonds por La Contraviesa
name_original: Polopos y Sorvilán – circular de los almendros por La Contraviesa
region_intro: Hiking around Polopos offers a blend of mountainous terrain and Mediterranean coastline in Andalusia. The village is situated on the southern slopes of the Sierra de la Contraviesa, characterized by ravines, vineyards, and almond groves. Its elevated position provides panoramic views of the sea and surrounding hills, connecting to the broader Alpujarra region. This diverse landscape provides a variety of trails for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Polopos The most popular hiking route is Polopos y Sorvilán – circular de los almonds por La Contraviesa, a 6.7 miles (10.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of almond blossoms in the Contraviesa mountains. Another top favourite among local hikers is Path of Silence loop from Viña Castilla, an easy 2.3 miles (3.6 km) path. This trail provides a relaxed walk through peaceful surroundings. Local hikers also love the View of Cala del Castell loop from Castell de Ferro, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 36 minutes. Hiking around Polopos is defined by mountainous terrain, Mediterranean coastline, and agricultural landscapes with vineyards and almond groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Polopos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 250 hikers have used komoot to explore Polopos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Polopos?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails around Polopos, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 13 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 1 challenging trail, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Polopos?
A: Hiking around Polopos offers a captivating blend of landscapes. You'll experience the mountainous terrain of the Sierra de la Contraviesa with its ravines, vineyards, and almond groves, especially beautiful during blossom season. The area also connects to the enchanting Alpujarra region, known for its valleys and traditional villages, and is only about 10 km from the Mediterranean coastline, providing stunning sea views and coastal paths.

Q: Are there any easy, relaxed walks suitable for beginners or a leisurely stroll?
A: Yes, Polopos offers several easy trails perfect for a relaxed walk. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33934993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path of Silence loop from Viña Castilla&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.6 km path. Another easy route with coastal views is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14599521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Cala del Castell loop from Castell de Ferro&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 5.0 km long.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Polopos area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Polopos are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979978132/polopos-y-sorvilan-circular-de-los-almonds-por-la-contraviesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polopos y Sorvilán – circular de los almonds por La Contraviesa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.7 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33934993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path of Silence loop from Viña Castilla&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.6 km loop. These allow you to start and end your hike in the same location without retracing your steps.

Q: What are some notable natural features or viewpoints to look out for on hikes?
A: Due to its elevated position, Polopos boasts numerous natural viewpoints offering breathtaking vistas of the Contraviesa mountains and the Mediterranean Sea. You might also encounter specific flora like the 'varica de San Jose' blooming in spring. The area is also known for its extensive almond groves, which are a beautiful sight, especially when in bloom. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Águila&lt;/a&gt; is a prominent summit in the region.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Polopos?
A: The trails in Polopos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from mountain views to coastal paths, and the opportunity to experience the tranquil beauty of the Sierra de la Contraviesa and the Alpujarra region.

Q: Are there any coastal walks or beaches accessible from Polopos?
A: Absolutely. Despite its mountain location, Polopos is only about 10 km from the coast. You can find beautiful coves and beaches nearby, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5531725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de la Rijana&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4257931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de Casarones&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38267122" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bicycle Repair Station – Torre del Cautor Beach loop from La Mamola&lt;/a&gt; offers an easy coastal walk.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can be found near the hiking routes?
A: Polopos itself is a traditional white Andalusian village with a rich Nasrid heritage, evident in its architecture and the 18th-century Church of Nuestra Señora del Rosario. The area also has a strong wine-growing tradition, with a Wine Museum ('El lagar del Cehelí'). Along the coast, you can explore historical structures like the Moorish-era Tower of Cautor and the Hornabeque de Baños fortification.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Polopos?
A: The region around Polopos is generally pleasant for hiking for much of the year. Spring (especially during almond blossom season) and autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Even winter can be suitable for hiking, given the Mediterranean climate, though higher elevations might be cooler. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during this period.

Q: Are there any specific trails known for almond blossoms?
A: Yes, the region is famous for its almond groves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979978132/polopos-y-sorvilan-circular-de-los-almonds-por-la-contraviesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Polopos y Sorvilán – circular de los almonds por La Contraviesa&lt;/a&gt; is specifically highlighted for offering views of almond blossoms in the Contraviesa mountains. The almond blossom season, typically in late winter to early spring, transforms the landscape into a stunning display of white and pink.

Q: Is Polopos part of the Alpujarra region, and what does that mean for hiking?
A: Yes, Polopos is considered part of the enchanting Alpujarra region. This means you'll find extensive hiking trails characterized by authentic villages, Moorish architecture, and stunning valleys and ravines. The Alpujarra offers a mountainous paradise between Granada and the Mediterranean, providing a rich cultural and natural backdrop for your hikes.
tips: Nice mountain town where you can taste local wines. It is considered part of the coastal towns of the Costa Tropical, so it has a unique climate. Its name derives from Latin and means 'poplars'.
On this path you will see many almond trees, which bloom at the end of winter, completely changing the image of the place. It is worth coming to see its flowering.
